# medus-react LLM Instructions (Frontend UI-Only)

This repository is a standalone admin web app focused on frontend UI.

## 1) Mission
Implement and improve UI/UX only. Keep work inside frontend boundaries.

## 2) Allowed Work
- React component development
- Page and route composition
- Layout and navigation updates
- Styling (Tailwind/CSS), spacing, typography, colors
- Dark/light theme behavior
- Accessibility improvements (labels, keyboard, focus)
- i18n string usage and locale-aware UI updates
- Mock data and frontend adapters needed to render UI

## 3) Disallowed Work
- Backend services, API server contracts, auth server logic
- Database setup, migrations, schemas, seed pipelines
- Queue/event infra, cloud/deployment, CI/CD ops changes
- Any Medusa backend integration requirement

## 4) Code Areas to Prioritize
- `src/components/**`
- `src/routes/**`
- `src/providers/**`
- `src/i18n/**`
- `src/lib/**` (frontend utilities and mocks)
- `public/**` for static frontend assets/docs

## 5) UX Quality Requirements
For each changed screen/flow, cover:
- Loading state
- Error state
- Empty state
- Success state

Also ensure:
- Accessible interactive controls
- Consistent spacing/typography with existing patterns
- Mobile + desktop layout sanity

## 6) Technical Constraints
- Prefer existing component patterns before creating new abstractions.
- Keep changes minimal and scoped.
- Do not introduce backend dependencies.
- Keep user-visible text translatable when project pattern supports it.

## 7) Build & Verification
Before considering work done:
1. Run `npm run build`.
2. Resolve UI-related TypeScript/build errors in touched areas.
3. Confirm no backend/API/DB/infra files were modified.

## 8) Response Behavior for LLM Agents
When asked to implement features:
- Propose frontend-only approach.
- If request implies backend work, redirect to mock/frontend alternative.
- Keep output actionable with file-level changes.
